payload.title: Ahead of Opening Day, Mamdani Administration Breaks Ground on Project to Improve Bronx Crosstown Bus Service and Safety Near Yankee Stadium
DDC Acting Commissioner
payload.agency_name: NYC Department of Design and Construction

payload.title: MTA New York City Transit Executive Vice President of Buses
New York City Council Majority Leader

payload.description: Bronx crosstown bus route serving East 161st Street corridor between Manhattan and the Bronx

payload.project_name: East 161st Street and Bx6 Capital Project
payload.description: Project to improve Bronx crosstown bus service and street safety near Yankee Stadium, adding westbound bus-only lanes, converting the 161st Street underpass to buses only, creating fully protected center-running bus lanes, pedestrian safety upgrades, and new amenities for bus riders
payload.partner_agency: NYC Department of Design and Construction

payload.description: Bronx crosstown corridor near Yankee Stadium, subject of capital project for bus lanes and safety improvements

payload.limits: Washington Avenue to Tiffany Street (includes segment Intervale Avenue to Tiffany Street)
payload.description: Portion of East 163rd Street included in the East 161st Street and Bx6 capital project
Mayor Mamdani, NYC DOT Commissioner Flynn, and DDC Acting Commissioner del Valle announced the start of a major project to improve Bronx crosstown bus service and street safety near Yankee Stadium
Construction is expected to continue through 2028
Construction has already begun on East 163rd Street between Intervale Avenue and Tiffany Street
Westbound bus-only lanes added as part of the project
Converting the 161st Street underpass to buses only
Fully protected, center-running bus lane along East 161st Street from Concourse Village West to just west of River Avenue
locations: East 161st Street from Concourse Village West to just west of River Avenue

locations_normalized.raw_text: East 161st Street from Concourse Village West to just west of River Avenue

payload.description: Upgrade existing eastbound bus tunnel under the Grand Concourse to bus-only use in both directions (currently restricted to eastbound buses only)
Three blocks of westbound 161st Street on either side of the Grand Concourse tunnel converted to bus-only
locations: Westbound 161st Street, three blocks on either side of the Grand Concourse tunnel

locations_normalized.raw_text: Westbound 161st Street, three blocks on either side of the Grand Concourse tunnel

payload.description: Full-length concrete boarding islands along center-running bus lanes to improve safety, shorten pedestrian crossing distances and prevent vehicles from blocking bus lanes
Sidewalk extensions (bus bulbs) at bus stops along the Bx6 route to speed up boarding and create space for seating and bus shelters
Bus shelters, benches and leaning bars for bus riders
Pedestrian refuges and infrastructure to make boarding buses safer and more accessible

Curb extensions, medians and pedestrian refuge islands to shorten crossing distances and improve visibility. Existing painted safety features upgraded to concrete extending sidewalk out to new curblines.
Rebuild, expand and landscape medians at East 161st Street and River Avenue, just outside of Yankee Stadium
City agencies have developed a traffic mitigation plan to minimize traffic disruptions during Yankees games
370 ADA-compliant pedestrian ramps
57 new trees and approximately 8,000 plantings of grass and perennials
More than 100 new street lighting and traffic signal poles installed
16 fire hydrants upgraded
Reconstruct about 45,000 square yards of roadway
Upgrade or add more than 180,000 square feet of sidewalk
More than 4,500 feet of water mains and sewers upgraded, additional catch basins added for drainage

payload.raw_value_text: 25,000 daily riders on the Bx6 Select Bus Service (SBS) line
more than three million visitors each year
Pedestrian refuge islands have been shown to reduce deaths and serious injuries by 35.5%
curb and sidewalk extensions reduce them by 34.1%
approximately 8,000 plantings of grass and perennials
More than 100 new street lighting and traffic signal poles
reconstruct about 45,000 square yards of roadway
more than 180,000 square feet of sidewalk
more than 4,500 feet of water mains and sewers

payload.entity_name: New York City Department of Transportation
NYC Department of Design and Construction
payload.agency_name: NYC Department of Design and Construction

payload.entity_name: NYC Department of Design and Construction
payload.agency_name: NYC Department of Design and Construction (DDC)

payload.claim_text: The project follows extensive community engagement and has support from local community boards
payload.description: Project has community board support

payload.claim_text: Project will deliver faster, safer and easier commutes for more than 25,000 daily bus riders
payload.description: Project benefits 25,000+ daily Bx6 SBS riders

payload.claim_text: The fully protected, center-running bus lane along East 161st Street is 'one of the only corridors of its kind in New York City'
payload.description: Center-running bus lane corridor is rare in NYC
